name: University of Cambridge
notes: Cambridge does not offer a standalone Chemical Engineering undergraduate degree; students apply to Engineering (H100) and specialise in chemical and biochemical engineering from Year 3. ENGAA required; interview at college. Chemistry A-level strongly recommended in addition to Maths and Physics.

name: Imperial College London
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Imperial is widely regarded as the UK's top standalone Chemical Engineering department. World-class research in reaction engineering, process systems and biochemical engineering. Major employers include BP, Shell, AstraZeneca and Procter & Gamble. Mathematics, Chemistry and Physics required.

name: University College London
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. UCL Chemical Engineering has particular strengths in biochemical engineering, process intensification and energy systems. Strong London employer network — BP, Shell and Dow Chemical are active recruiters. Mathematics and Chemistry required.

name: University of Manchester
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Manchester's Centre for Process Integration is internationally recognised. Strong links to ICI legacy and the UK chemical and petrochemical industries in the North West. Mathematics and Chemistry required at A-level.

name: University of Edinburgh
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Edinburgh Chemical Engineering has strong oil and gas process engineering expertise (proximity to North Sea industry). 4-year Scottish BEng or 5-year MEng pathway. Mathematics and Chemistry required; Physics recommended.

name: University of Bath
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Bath Chemical Engineering consistently achieves excellent graduate employment through its mandatory placement year. Placement employers include AstraZeneca, Pfizer, Procter & Gamble, Kimberly-Clark and Unilever. Mathematics and Chemistry required.

name: University of Birmingham
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Birmingham Chemical Engineering has particular strengths in biochemical engineering, reaction engineering and sustainable process design. Proximity to the Midlands chemical industry corridor. Mathematics and Chemistry required.

name: University of Sheffield
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Sheffield Chemical Engineering is strong in particle technology, combustion and sustainable energy. Grantham Centre for Sustainable Futures links environmental sustainability to chemical process design. Mathematics and Chemistry required.

name: University of Leeds
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Leeds Chemical Engineering is internationally known for food engineering and particle technology. Institute of Process Research and Development (iPRD) bridges industrial chemistry and chemical engineering. Mathematics and Chemistry required.

name: University of Nottingham
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Nottingham has proximity to AstraZeneca (major graduate employer) and is strong in pharmaceutical process engineering and bioprocess engineering. Mathematics and Chemistry required.

name: Newcastle University
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Newcastle Chemical Engineering has strong process engineering and energy systems groups. The North East's chemical industry (Teesside chemical cluster) provides excellent industry links. Mathematics and Chemistry required.

name: Loughborough University
notes: UCAS 2026 verified title match. Loughborough Chemical Engineering is strong in bioprocessing, water treatment and sustainable energy. Excellent industry placement record with FMCG and energy companies. Mathematics and Chemistry required.
